<!--
  Thanks for contributing to Firstline / Atlas!
  Please use a Conventional Commit style title, e.g. `feat(api): add breach timeline export`.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do, and why? Link any related issues with "Closes #123". -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply. -->

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` — code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- [ ] `perf` — performance improvement
- [ ] `test` — adding or fixing tests
- [ ] `build` / `ci` — build system or CI changes
- [ ] `chore` — other changes that don't modify src or test files
- [ ] Breaking change (note it in the title with `!` and explain below)

## How tested

<!-- Commands run, environments covered, manual steps, screenshots/logs as relevant. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests pass locally (`pytest` / `vitest` / `terraform validate` as applicable)
- [ ] Lint and types are clean (`ruff`, `mypy`, `tsc` / ESLint)
- [ ] Docs updated where needed (README, `docs/`, code comments)
- [ ] No secrets, credentials, or customer data committed
- [ ] PR title follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)
